title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Vomiting
intro_desc: If you're experiencing vomiting, herbal essential oils might be worth trying. These natural remedies can help soothe your stomach and get your digestive system back on track. So, why do they work? Herbal teas, like those made from Zingiber officinale (ginger), Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root), and Curcuma longa (turmeric), contain compounds that can calm your stomach and reduce inflammation. For instance, ginger's active compound, gingerol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease nausea and vomiting. Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which can help stabilize your stomach acid levels and prevent further vomiting. Turmeric, on the other hand, contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce inflammation in your digestive tract. By using these herbal essential oils, you can experience several benefits, such as reduced nausea, fewer stomach cramps, and a faster recovery time. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can also develop a healthier relationship with food and reduce your reliance on medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by relaxing the digestive tract muscles. Another key constituent, shogaol, has a direct effect on the brain's vomiting center, reducing the urge to vomit. The essential oils' spicy and warming properties also stimulate digestion and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate vomiting caused by poor digestion. By reducing inflammation and calming the stomach, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh ginger roots, a clean glass jar, a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, a bowl, and a dark glass bottle.
Wash the ginger roots with 1 cup of water, then chop them into small pieces and put them in the glass jar.
Cover the jar with the cheesecloth or coffee filter and let it sit in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid into the bowl through the cheesecloth or coffee filter, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to the dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in water for vomiting relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The essential oil also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage. These properties help to calm the stomach and reduce vomiting by reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils work together to help alleviate vomiting and other digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, and a clean glass bottle.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) to the glass jar and add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Steep the roots in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for skin application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its healing properties. Curcumin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues. Curcuma longa essential oils also contains gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-nausea properties. The oils' properties help to calm the stomach and reduce vomiting by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin also help to reduce inflammation in the stomach, making it an effective remedy for vomiting caused by digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Curcuma longa rhizomes, 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive), and a clean glass jar.
Wash the rhizomes with water, then dry them with a paper towel.
Grate the dried rhizomes using a cheese grater or a food processor.
Combine the grated rhizomes with the carrier oil in the glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a compound called anethole, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. These properties help to soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing the frequency and severity of vomiting. The oil also contains estragole, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate bowel movements and reduce nausea. The anti-spasmodic properties of Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can also help to alleviate cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines, further reducing the likelihood of vomiting. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Heat 2 cups of water in a saucepan and add the Foeniculum vulgare seeds. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the seeds to get all the liquid.
Mix the liquid with the 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds in a clean bowl. Let it sit for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture again through a cheesecloth into a glass bottle. Discard the seeds and store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils contains quinine, a well-known compound that has been used to treat vomiting for centuries. Quinine has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating vomiting. Quinine also has a sedative effect on the digestive system, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains other bioactive constituents, such as alkaloids like cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can further help to alleviate vomiting. By reducing inflammation, calming the stomach, and easing muscle spasms, Cinchona officinalis essential oils can help to effectively manage vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis leaves. Crush them into small pieces.
Place the crushed leaves in a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Filter the essential oil one more time through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a small glass bottle. Use within 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe the digestive system. Cinnamaldehyde also has a compound called eugenol, which has antimicrobial properties and can help to reduce nausea and vomiting. The essential oil's warm and spicy aroma can also help to stimulate digestion and relieve feelings of queasiness. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria in the digestive tract, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can help to alleviate vomiting and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for vomiting

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains menthone, limonene, and menthol as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea. Menthone and limonene have been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help to eliminate the bacteria that cause vomiting. Menthol, on the other hand, has a cooling effect on the stomach, which can help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. By reducing inflammation and eliminating bacteria, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help to alleviate vomiting and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water.
Chop the leaves finely and place them in a saucepan with the water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Store the extracted liquid in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid and label it as 'Peppermint oil for vomiting' (dilute with a carrier oil before using).

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber cassumunar essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it easier to manage vomiting. The oil also contains a compound called zingiberene, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the essential oil contains a compound called shogaol, which has been found to have a strong anti-emetic effect, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting. By using Zingiber cassumunar essential oils, individuals can experience relief from vomiting due to its anti-inflammatory, anti-emetic, and calming properties.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber cassumunar herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber cassumunar roots, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a glass jar with a lid.
Clean the Zingiber cassumunar roots with water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the Zingiber cassumunar roots and carrier oil in the glass jar. Use 1 tablespoon of roots for every 2 tablespoons of oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Discard the Zingiber cassumunar roots and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for external use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Elettaria cardamomum essential oils contains compounds like limonene, linalool, and 1,8-cineole. These active constituents have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve nausea. The essential oils also have a sedative effect, which can calm the stomach and reduce the frequency of vomiting. The presence of limonene and linalool can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and improve digestion. By reducing inflammation and calming the stomach, Elettaria cardamomum essential oils may help to alleviate vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Elettaria cardamomum herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Get 2 cups of Elettaria cardamomum seeds. Clean and dry them.
Use a coffee grinder or spice grinder to crush the seeds into a fine powder.
Transfer the powder to a glass jar. Add 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Strain the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 2-3 drops as needed to help relieve nausea and vomiting.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils contains compounds like borneol, limonene, and beta-pinene. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe stomach cramps and reduce nausea. The oil's analgesic and anxiolytic properties can also help to calm the mind and body, reducing the frequency and severity of vomiting episodes. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties can aid in relieving digestive discomfort and clearing congestion. By using Piper nigrum essential oils, some people may find relief from vomiting due to its ability to regulate digestive function and alleviate symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Piper nigrum seeds and 4 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Heat 4 cups of carrier oil in a double boiler or a saucepan with a bowl on top.
Add 1 cup of Piper nigrum seeds to the carrier oil and heat for 2-3 hours or until the oil turns red.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Store the Piper nigrum essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 2-3 drops as needed to help with vomiting.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains the bioactive constituents aloe-emodin, aloin, and aloe-annoside. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. Aloin, in particular, has been shown to have a stabilizing effect on gut contractions and may help alleviate vomiting caused by stomach upset. Aloe-emodin also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that may contribute to vomiting. By reducing inflammation and stabilizing gut contractions, Aloe barbadensis essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of vomiting and promote a sense of calm in the digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for vomiting
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Aloe barbadensis gel and 1 cup of carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil.
Chop 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil and add it to the Aloe gel.
Mix 1 teaspoon of peppermint essential oil with 1 teaspoon of ginger essential oil.
Combine the Aloe gel mixture with the essential oil mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a bottle and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use 5-7 drops as needed for vomiting.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent vomiting in some cases. Peppermint tea, for example, has a calming effect on the stomach and can ease nausea. Ginger tea is also known to reduce nausea and vomiting. These teas work by soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate vomiting.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for vomiting every day can be safe, but it depends on the type of tea and your individual health. Some teas, like ginger and peppermint, are gentle and might help. However, teas containing other herbs like senna or licorice root can be too strong and cause problems if used daily.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile may help with vomiting. The effects can start within 10-30 minutes after drinking the tea. This is because these herbs can calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The severity of vomiting and individual tolerance may affect how quickly the tea works, but some people find relief within a half hour.
faq_time: When you're feeling queasy, a soothing herbal tea can help calm your stomach. A good time to try it is in the morning, before you eat or drink anything else. Drinking it on an empty stomach can help the tea work its way through your system and ease any nausea.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for vomiting. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger can speed up digestion, which might worsen vomiting. Other herbs like licorice root and chamomile can slow down stomach emptying, which could also affect medication absorption and worsen vomiting symptoms. This interaction can vary depending on the specific herbal tea and medication.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and vomiting, herbal teas might sound like a good idea to calm your stomach. However, some herbal teas can actually worsen your condition. Peppermint and ginger teas are often recommended, but it's essential to check the ingredients and make sure they're safe for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a natural way to calm nausea while breastfeeding. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. For example, peppermint and ginger teas are often used to ease morning sickness, but in large amounts, they might cause digestive issues in the baby.
title_2: Soothing Vomiting in Infants with 11 Essential Oils
